scope_note: QA Fibonacci Matrix Orbit Periods cert [291]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) Fibonacci primitive roots and the period of the Fibonacci sequence modulo a prime, American Mathematical Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano period pi(m) = order of Fibonacci matrix in GL(2,Z/mZ)); Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (QA T-operator, BEDA tuples, orbit classification). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For QA mod m=9, the Fibonacci matrix M=[[0,1],[1,1]] acting on (Z/9Z)^2 by (b,e)->M*(b,e) has order exactly 24 in GL(2,Z/9Z) (= Pisano period pi(9)); no proper divisor of 24 is the order. The three orbit types partition {1,...,9}^2 exactly: (1) Singularity: {(9,9)}, period 1, M*(0,0)=(0,0) mod 9; (2) Satellite: {(b,e): 3|b AND 3|e} \ {(9,9)}, 8 states, period 8 each; (3) Cosmos: remaining 72 states, period 24 each. Five-families alignment: Fibonacci/Lucas/Phibonacci sequence pairs have period 24 (Cosmos); Tribonacci period 8 (Satellite); Ninbonacci period 1 (Singularity). Exhaustive: all 81 states checked. Theorem NT: matrix entries and orbit computations are integer arithmetic; no float state. Mechanism chain: cert [289] (Koenig Pell Ford); cert [290] (Classical Subfamily Ford Cusps); cert [281] (QA Pisano-Orbit Correspondence). Lineage: derived 2026-06-01.
